habitat: Subtropical evergreen broadleaf forest; forest edges; open woodland; and wooded foothills
story: The Pied Falconet (Microhierax melanoleucos) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Falconidae family. It is most commonly observed in Subtropical evergreen broadleaf forest; forest edges; open woodland; and wooded foothills, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Least Concern (LC) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Pied Falconet is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Pied Falconet into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
